qr_title: Free QR Code Generator — Create QR Codes Instantly
qr_description: Create and download high-quality QR codes for URLs, text, WiFi, and more — all in your browser, 100% free.
qr_placeholder_text: Enter text or a URL above and click Generate QR Code
qr_error_empty: Please enter a URL or text to generate a QR code.
qr_error_failed: Failed to generate QR code. Please check your input and try again.
qr_error_download: Failed to download QR code. Please try again.
qr_error_no_code: Please generate a QR code first.
qr_seo_title: Create QR Codes Instantly — No Sign-Up Required
qr_intro_3: Unlike other online QR generators, we don't watermark your codes, impose download limits, or redirect through tracking servers. Everything runs directly in your browser — your data never touches a server.
qr_how_title: How to Use the QR Code Generator
qr_step_1_desc: Type or paste the URL, text, phone number, or any other data you want to encode into the input field above. For example: https://example.com.
qr_step_2_desc: Choose your preferred size (Small, Medium, Large, or Extra Large) and pick custom colors for both the QR code pattern and its background using the color pickers.
qr_step_3_desc: Click the "Generate QR Code" button (or toggle Auto-generate to create codes instantly as you type). Your QR code will appear in the output area below.
qr_step_4_desc: Use your phone's camera or a QR scanning app to verify the code works. Test it before printing or sharing to ensure it redirects to the correct destination.
qr_step_5_desc: Click the "Download PNG" button to save your QR code as a high-resolution PNG image. Use it on websites, flyers, business cards, posters, menus, or anywhere you need a scannable code.
qr_faq_q1: Can I generate QR codes for free?
qr_faq_a1: Absolutely! Our QR code generator is 100% free with no hidden charges, no sign-up requirements, and no limits on the number of codes you can create. Generate as many QR codes as you need, for any purpose — personal or commercial.
qr_faq_a2: Currently, we offer high-quality PNG download. PNG is a lossless raster format that works perfectly for both digital use (websites, social media, emails) and print (flyers, posters, business cards). The generated images have a clean white background with your chosen QR color, making them easy to place on any design.
qr_faq_q3: Do you store my data or track my usage?
qr_faq_a3: No. Everything runs entirely in your browser using JavaScript. The text or URL you enter never leaves your device — there are no server requests, no tracking, and no data logging. Your privacy is completely protected.
qr_faq_q4: Can I change the colors of the QR code?
qr_faq_a4: Yes! Use the color pickers in the options panel to change both the QR code pattern color and the background color. This lets you match your QR code to your brand colors or design theme. Just make sure there's enough contrast between the two colors for reliable scanning.
qr_faq_q5: What kind of content can I encode?
qr_faq_a5: You can encode any text content — URLs (links), plain text, phone numbers, email addresses, WiFi credentials (in network configuration format), contact information (vCard), calendar events, geographic coordinates, and more. For best results with different content types, ensure your input uses the correct format (e.g., mailto:email@example.com for email, tel:+1234567890 for phone numbers).
qr_related_desc: Try these other free tools from our collection.
qr_related_1_desc: Create strong, secure passwords with custom length and character options.
qr_related_2_desc: Convert images between PNG, JPG, WebP, and more — all in your browser.
qr_related_3_desc: Convert length, weight, temperature, volume, and more in a tap.
wu_description: Enter scrambled letters to find all valid English words — perfect for Scrabble, Words With Friends, and crosswords.
wu_no_results: No words found. Try different letters or adjust your filters.
wu_error_empty: Please enter some letters to unscramble.
wu_seo_title: Word Unscrambler — Find Words from Scrambled Letters
wu_intro_3: Our word unscrambler uses a built-in dictionary of nearly 10,000 common English words — no internet connection needed, so your privacy is protected. Just type in your scrambled letters, apply optional filters like minimum word length or prefix/suffix matching, and instantly see all possible words sorted from longest to shortest. Each result is grouped by word length so you can easily spot the highest-scoring plays.
wu_how_title: How to Use the Word Unscrambler
wu_step_1_desc: Type or paste the letters into the input field. For example, enter TRAE to find all words that can be formed from those letters. Letters are automatically capitalized for clarity.
wu_step_2_desc: Set a minimum word length to exclude very short words. Use the Starts With, Ends With, and Contains fields to narrow your search — perfect for crossword clues.
wu_step_3_desc: Press the button or hit Enter on your keyboard. The tool will search its built-in dictionary and display all matching words.
wu_step_4_desc: Words are grouped by length, with the longest words shown first. The word count and longest word are displayed at the top of the results.
wu_step_5_desc: Click "Copy All" to copy every found word to your clipboard as a comma-separated list. Hover over any word to highlight it — great for learning new vocabulary.
wu_faq_q1: Is this word unscrambler free to use?
wu_faq_a1: Yes, completely free! There are no usage limits, no sign-up requirements, and no hidden charges. Use it as many times as you need for Scrabble, Words With Friends, crosswords, or any word game.
wu_faq_a2: Yes! The dictionary of nearly 10,000 words is built directly into the page. Once the page has loaded, no internet connection is required — all processing happens in your browser. Your letters are never sent to a server.
wu_faq_q3: What dictionary does the unscrambler use?
wu_faq_a3: We use a curated list of approximately 10,000 common English words, sourced from public-domain word frequency lists. It includes standard vocabulary suitable for word games like Scrabble and Words With Friends, but may not include very obscure or technical terms.
wu_faq_q4: Can I find words with specific letters in certain positions?
wu_faq_a4: Absolutely! Use the filter fields below the input: Starts With finds words beginning with certain letters, Ends With finds words ending with certain letters, and Contains finds words containing a specific letter sequence anywhere inside them. Combine these with the minimum length filter for precise results.
wu_faq_q5: What's the best strategy for winning at Scrabble?
wu_faq_a5: Focus on finding the longest word possible, as longer words generally score higher. Pay attention to high-value letters (J, Q, X, Z) and look for prefixes and suffixes you can add to existing words on the board. Our unscrambler sorts results by length so you can spot the best plays instantly.

img_description: Convert images between PNG, JPG, WebP, and more. All processing happens in your browser — nothing is uploaded.
img_convert_preview: Convert an image to see the result
img_error_unsupported: Unsupported file format. Please upload PNG, JPG, WebP, SVG, BMP, or GIF.
img_error_load: Failed to load image. The file may be corrupted.
img_error_read: Failed to read the file. Please try again.
img_error_dimensions: Dimensions must be at least 1 pixel.
img_error_convert: Conversion failed. The selected format may not be supported by your browser.
img_seo_title: Image Converter — Convert PNG, JPG, WebP and More
img_intro_3: You can also resize images during conversion by setting custom width and height values while maintaining the original aspect ratio. A quality slider gives you fine-grained control over JPG and WebP output compression, balancing file size with visual fidelity.
img_step_1_desc: Drag and drop an image file onto the upload area, or click to browse your computer. We support PNG, JPG, WebP, SVG, BMP, and GIF formats.
img_step_2_desc: Select your desired format from the dropdown: PNG (lossless, supports transparency), JPG (smaller file size, good for photos), or WebP (modern format with superior compression).
img_step_3_desc: Use the quality slider to control compression for JPG and WebP outputs. Optionally enter custom width and height values to resize the image. The aspect ratio lock keeps proportions intact.
img_step_4_desc: Click the "Convert" button to process your image. The conversion happens instantly in your browser using the Canvas API. A side-by-side preview shows the original and converted versions.
img_step_5_desc: Review the converted preview and compare file sizes. When you're satisfied, click the "Download" button to save the converted image to your computer.
img_faq_q1: Does this tool upload my images to a server?
img_faq_a1: No, never. All image processing happens entirely in your browser using the HTML5 Canvas API. Your images are never uploaded, stored, or transmitted anywhere. This makes the converter completely private and secure — ideal for sensitive or personal images.
img_faq_q2: Are there any file size limits?
img_faq_a2: There are no artificial limits imposed by the tool. However, very large images (e.g., 20,000+ pixel dimensions) may cause performance issues in your browser's Canvas API since all processing is done in memory. For typical photos and web graphics, the converter handles them with ease.
img_faq_q3: What's the difference between PNG, JPG, and WebP?
img_faq_a3: PNG is a lossless format that supports transparency, making it ideal for logos, icons, and graphics with sharp edges. JPG is a lossy format that produces smaller file sizes, best for photographs and complex images. WebP is a modern format developed by Google that offers both lossless and lossy compression — typically 25-35% smaller file sizes than JPG at equivalent quality, with support for transparency.
img_faq_a4: Converting between lossless formats (PNG to PNG, BMP to PNG) preserves full quality. Converting to a lossy format like JPG or WebP may reduce quality depending on the compression level you choose — the quality slider lets you control this trade-off. We recommend setting quality to 90% for a good balance of file size and visual fidelity.
img_faq_a5: Yes! You can upload SVG (vector) files and convert them to PNG, JPG, or WebP raster formats. The SVG is rendered onto a canvas and then exported in your chosen format. Note that the output will be a raster image at the specified resolution, not a vector file.

bmi_description: Calculate your Body Mass Index instantly. Get your BMI category, healthy weight range, and personalized health recommendations.
bmi_error_valid_height: Please enter a valid height in centimeters.
bmi_error_valid_weight: Please enter a valid weight in kilograms.
bmi_error_valid_height_ft: Please enter a valid height in feet.
bmi_error_valid_weight_lbs: Please enter a valid weight in pounds.
bmi_error_height_high: Height seems too high. Please check your input.
bmi_error_weight_high: Weight seems too high. Please check your input.
bmi_seo_title: BMI Calculator — Calculate Your Body Mass Index
bmi_how_title: How to Use the BMI Calculator
bmi_step_1_desc: Click Metric to enter height in centimeters and weight in kilograms, or Imperial to enter height in feet/inches and weight in pounds.
bmi_step_2_desc: Type your height in the provided field. In metric mode, enter centimeters (e.g., 175). In imperial mode, enter feet (e.g., 5'10" as feet=5 and inches=10).
bmi_step_3_desc: Enter your current weight — kilograms in metric mode, pounds in imperial mode. The input accepts decimal values for precise measurements.
bmi_step_4_desc: Press the "Calculate BMI" button or hit Enter on your keyboard. Your BMI value, category, and healthy weight range will appear instantly.
bmi_step_5_desc: Review your BMI on the color-coded scale and read the health implications for your category. Use the "Share Results" button to save or share your BMI with your healthcare provider.
bmi_faq_q2: Is BMI accurate for athletes and bodybuilders?
bmi_faq_q3: What is the ideal BMI for my age?
bmi_faq_q4: Does BMI account for gender differences?
bmi_faq_q5: How can I lower my BMI?
bmi_faq_q6: Can BMI be inaccurate for certain ethnic groups?
bmi_cat_underweight_risk: Increased risk of malnutrition, osteoporosis, anemia
bmi_cat_normal_risk: Lowest risk of weight-related health problems
bmi_cat_overweight_risk: Moderate risk of heart disease, diabetes, hypertension
bmi_cat_obese_risk: High risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, stroke
bmi_health_underweight: Underweight (< 18.5): Being underweight may indicate malnutrition, digestive disorders, or other underlying health conditions. It can lead to weakened immune function, osteoporosis, and fertility issues. If your BMI falls in this range, consider consulting a healthcare provider to identify the cause and develop a plan to achieve a healthy weight.
bmi_health_normal: Normal (18.5 – 24.9): A BMI in this range is associated with the lowest risk of weight-related health problems. However, it's important to maintain a balanced diet and regular physical activity to support overall health, regardless of BMI. This range is considered optimal for most adults.
bmi_health_overweight: Overweight (25 – 29.9): Excess weight increases the risk of developing chronic conditions such as heart disease, type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure, and certain cancers. Even modest weight loss (5-10% of body weight) can significantly reduce these risks and improve metabolic health.
bmi_health_obese: Obese (30+): Obesity is a serious health condition that substantially increases the risk of cardiovascular disease, stroke, type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, joint problems, and several types of cancer. If your BMI is 30 or higher, it's strongly recommended to consult a healthcare professional for a comprehensive assessment and personalized weight management plan.
bmi_limitations_intro: While BMI is a useful screening tool, it has several important limitations that should be considered when interpreting your results:
bmi_limit_muscle: Muscle mass: Athletes and people with high muscle mass may have a high BMI despite having very low body fat. Muscle weighs more than fat, so BMI can overestimate body fat in muscular individuals.
bmi_limit_age: Age: Older adults typically have more body fat than younger adults at the same BMI. BMI does not account for age-related changes in body composition.
bmi_limit_gender: Gender: Women generally have a higher percentage of body fat than men at the same BMI. The same BMI cutoffs are used for both genders, which may not accurately reflect health risks for everyone.
bmi_limit_ethnicity: Ethnicity: People of Asian descent may have higher health risks at a lower BMI compared to other populations. Some studies suggest that the healthy BMI range for Asian populations should be lower (18.5 – 23).
bmi_limit_composition: Body composition: BMI does not distinguish between fat mass and lean mass, nor does it indicate where fat is distributed in the body. Abdominal fat (belly fat) is more strongly linked to health risks than fat stored in other areas.

pw_description: Create strong, secure passwords with full control over length, character types, and complexity.
pw_error_no_type: Please select at least one character type.
pw_error_copy: Failed to copy. Please select and copy manually.
pw_seo_title: Password Generator — Create Strong, Secure Passwords
pw_intro_3: Our free password generator creates cryptographically random passwords based on your chosen settings. Select the length, enable the character types you need, and optionally exclude ambiguous characters that are easy to misread (like 0, O, I, and l). The built-in strength meter gives you real-time feedback on your password's security level.
pw_how_title: How to Use the Password Generator
pw_step_1_desc: Drag the slider to choose a length between 8 and 64 characters. Longer passwords are more secure — we recommend at least 16 characters for strong protection.
pw_step_2_desc: Check the boxes for the character sets you want to include: uppercase letters (A–Z), lowercase letters (a–z), numbers (0–9), and symbols (!@#$%^&*). Using all types maximizes password strength.
pw_step_3_desc: Enable the exclusion option to remove characters that look similar, such as zero and the letter O, or uppercase I and lowercase l. This makes passwords easier to read and type.
pw_step_4_desc: Click "Generate Password" to create a random password. The password appears in the display above, and the strength meter updates to show its security level.
pw_step_5_desc: Click the copy icon (📋) to copy the password to your clipboard. Use the refresh icon (🔄) to quickly generate a new one. Store your passwords in a password manager for safekeeping.
pw_guide_weak_desc: Short passwords with few character types. Can be cracked in seconds or minutes. Not recommended for any account.
pw_guide_medium_desc: Moderate length with mixed characters. May withstand basic attacks but is vulnerable to determined hackers. Suitable for low-risk accounts.
pw_guide_strong_desc: Long passwords with all character types enabled. Would take years or centuries to crack with current technology. Recommended for all accounts.
pw_faq_a1: A strong password has three key attributes: length (at least 12–16 characters), complexity (a mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols), and unpredictability (no dictionary words, personal info, or common patterns). Our generator ensures all three by creating truly random passwords with your chosen settings.
pw_faq_q2: How long should my password be?
pw_faq_a2: We recommend a minimum of 12 characters for most accounts, and 16 characters or more for sensitive accounts like email, banking, and password managers. Each additional character exponentially increases the time required to crack the password — a 20-character password with mixed characters would take billions of years to brute-force with current technology.
pw_faq_q3: Should I use the same password for multiple accounts?
pw_faq_a3: No, never. Using the same password across multiple accounts means that if one account is compromised, all your other accounts are immediately at risk. Always use a unique password for every account. A password manager can help you generate and store hundreds of unique passwords without having to memorize them.
pw_faq_q4: What are ambiguous characters and should I exclude them?
pw_faq_a4: Ambiguous characters are letters and numbers that look similar to each other, such as 0 (zero) and O (letter O), or I (uppercase i) and l (lowercase L). If you'll be reading or typing the password manually, excluding these characters reduces confusion. If you're using a password manager (which we recommend), you can safely include them.
pw_faq_q5: Is this password generator truly random?
pw_faq_a5: Yes. The generator uses JavaScript's crypto.getRandomValues() API, which is cryptographically secure and designed specifically for security-sensitive applications. This is the same underlying technology used by web browsers for encryption — far more secure than the basic Math.random() function, which should never be used for password generation.
pw_faq_q6: How should I store my generated passwords?
pw_faq_a6: Never store passwords in plain text files, sticky notes, or unencrypted documents. We strongly recommend using a password manager like Bitwarden, 1Password, or Apple Keychain. These tools encrypt your passwords and sync them across your devices. Many password managers also include built-in generators and auto-fill features that make using strong passwords effortless.

uc_description: Instantly convert between hundreds of units — length, weight, temperature, volume, and speed.
uc_seo_title: Unit Converter — Convert Length, Weight, Temperature & More
uc_intro_3: Behind the scenes, every conversion uses precise formulas and conversion factors. Temperature conversions handle the different scaling (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin), while length, weight, volume, and speed all use consistent multiplicative factors with high precision.
uc_how_title: How to Use the Unit Converter
uc_step_1_desc: Click on one of the category pills — Length, Weight, Temperature, Volume, or Speed. The unit dropdowns will update to show relevant units for that category.
uc_step_2_desc: Type or paste the number you want to convert in the Value field. Decimal values are supported (e.g., 1.5, 3.14).
uc_step_3_desc: Select the unit you're converting from in the first dropdown, and the unit you want to convert to in the second dropdown. Use the swap button (⇄) to quickly reverse them.
uc_step_4_desc: The converted value appears instantly below with full precision formatting. A conversion formula reference is shown beneath the result.
uc_step_5_desc: Your recent conversions are saved in the history list. Click on any history item to re-run that conversion, or click Clear all to reset.
uc_faq_q1: How many units does this converter support?
uc_faq_a1: The converter supports over 60 units across five categories: length (19 units), weight/mass (15 units), temperature (3 scales), volume (15 units), and speed (10 units). From common units like meters and feet to specialized ones like nautical miles and knots.
uc_faq_a2: Yes! Temperature conversions use the exact formulas: °F = (°C × 9/5) + 32, °C = (°F − 32) × 5/9, K = °C + 273.15. Results are displayed with up to 2 decimal places for precision.
uc_faq_q3: Can I convert between metric and imperial?
uc_faq_a3: Absolutely. Every category includes both metric and imperial units (and sometimes others like nautical or scientific units). Simply select your source unit from the first dropdown and your target unit from the second — the converter handles the math automatically.
uc_faq_q4: Does it save my recent conversions?
uc_faq_a4: Yes! Your last 15 conversions are stored in the history panel below the converter. Click on any history entry to instantly re-run that conversion. History is cleared when you close the page or tap Clear all.
uc_faq_q5: How are the conversion factors determined?
uc_faq_a5: All conversion factors (except temperature) are based on their relationship to a standard base unit (meter for length, kilogram for weight, liter for volume, m/s for speed). Each unit stores its multiplier relative to that base. When converting, the tool converts from the source unit to the base, then from the base to the target unit, ensuring maximum precision.
uc_related_desc: Try these other free tools from our collection.
